mission.title: Install and Configure Kubeelasti on Kubernetes
mission.description: KubeElasti is a Kubernetes-native solution that enables scale-to-zero functionality, allowing services to scale down during periods of low or no traffic without losing requests. Installing KubeElasti helps optimize resource usage and reduce costs while maintaining service availability.

steps.title: Create KubeElasti Namespace
steps.description:
Create a dedicated namespace for KubeElasti to run in. Run the following command to create the namespace:
```bash
kubectl create namespace kubeelasti
```

steps.title: Deploy KubeElasti Components
steps.description:
Apply the KubeElasti manifests to deploy the components. Use the following commands:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/truefoundry/KubeElasti/v1.0.0/deploy/kubeelasti-controller.yaml -n kubeelasti
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/truefoundry/KubeElasti/v1.0.0/deploy/kubeelasti-webhook.yaml -n kubeelasti
```

steps.title: Verify Installation
steps.description:
Check if the KubeElasti pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubeelasti
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of KubeElasti will show the pods running in the `kubeelasti` namespace. You can further verify that the service is operational by checking the logs or accessing the service endpoints as needed.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/truefoundry/KubeElasti/v1.0.0/deploy/kubeelasti-controller.yaml -n kubeelasti
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/truefoundry/KubeElasti/v1.0.0/deploy/kubeelasti-webhook.yaml -n kubeelasti

uninstall.title: Remove KubeElasti
uninstall.description:
To uninstall KubeElasti, delete the resources applied in the namespace:
```bash
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/truefoundry/KubeElasti/v1.0.0/deploy/kubeelasti-controller.yaml -n kubeelasti
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/truefoundry/KubeElasti/v1.0.0/deploy/kubeelasti-webhook.yaml -n kubeelasti
kubectl delete namespace kubeelasti
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeElasti
upgrade.description:
To upgrade KubeElasti, apply the new manifests from the latest release. Ensure to replace the version in the URLs:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/truefoundry/KubeElasti/v1.1.0/deploy/kubeelasti-controller.yaml -n kubeelasti
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/truefoundry/KubeElasti/v1.1.0/deploy/kubeelasti-webhook.yaml -n kubeelasti
```

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the KubeElasti pods are not starting, check the pod logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kubeelasti
```

troubleshooting.title: Resource Quotas
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure that your cluster has enough resources available. Check the resource quotas and limits set in the namespace:
```bash
kubectl describe quota -n kubeelasti
```

troubleshooting.title: Network Issues
troubleshooting.description:
If you experience connectivity issues, verify that the services are correctly exposed and reachable:
```bash
kubectl get svc -n kubeelasti
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed and configured to communicate with your cluster.
